title: carex × pseudomairei
intro: Carex × pseudomairei, also known as the hybrid sedge, is a plant species that is a cross between Carex pseudoscyphia and Carex mairei. It is a type of perennial grass-like plant native to the mountainous regions of France. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can be attributed to the presence of certain flavonoids and phenolic compounds. Carex × pseudomairei can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory infections.
